participants.name: Nadia (0wnesr)
participants.professional_persona: Nadia is a Principal Research Scientist at a multinational corporation's AI lab in Singapore (e.g., Salesforce Research). She leads a team working on natural language understanding for enterprise applications and participates in 100E.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in large language models, dialogue systems, and knowledge graphs. Proven skill in product-oriented research that improves enterprise productivity and customer engagement.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Aspires to become a director of an industry AI lab in Singapore. Wants to collaborate with government agencies to develop AI solutions for public good.

participants.name: A (mdqo8d)
participants.entity_name: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-mdqo8dza
participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-mdqo8dza
participants.professional_persona: A 48-year-old nurse manager at a public hospital who has seen firsthand how AI-assisted diagnostics can improve patient outcomes. She is keen to ensure that AI in healthcare remains safe and equitable.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Clinical nursing expertise, hospital operations, and experience with electronic health records. Attended a workshop on AI ethics in healthcare.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: To lead a multi-disciplinary committee on AI deployment in her hospital and help draft guidelines for responsible AI use in nursing.

participants.name: A (yz4m1k)
participants.professional_persona: A mid-career data scientist in the logistics sector, I lead a team that develops predictive models for supply chain optimisation. Having participated in the 100 Experiments programme, I bridge the gap between operational pain points and AI-driven solutions.
participants.skills_and_expertise: I specialise in machine learning, time-series forecasting, and cloud-based deployment using tools like TensorFlow and Google Cloud AI. My background includes building end-to-end AI pipelines for real-time inventory management.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: I aim to become a chief AI officer within an enterprise, driving large-scale AI adoption across industries. I am also keen to mentor junior practitioners through company-led training programmes like TeSA's AIAP.

participants.name: Dr. (xeqo2m)
participants.professional_persona: Dr. Sim is a Research Fellow at SMU's Living Analytics Research Centre specialising in AI for public policy. He collaborates with government agencies to apply AI to Smart Nation priorities.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Expert in causal inference, network analysis, and social impact evaluation. Adept at communicating complex AI findings to non-technical policy makers.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: Seeks to build a bridge between AI research and public sector decision-making. Wants to co-author policy guidelines on responsible AI deployment.

participants.name: A (9r34v3)
participants.entity_name: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-9r34v3rv
participants.representative_type: AI for Public Good Principle | KG-Gen-9r34v3rv
participants.professional_persona: A 23-year-old recent graduate in AI from NUS who is about to join a government-linked AI lab. He wants to work on projects that directly benefit Singaporean society, such as public transport optimisation.
participants.skills_and_expertise: Strong foundation in deep learning, reinforcement learning, and software engineering. Completed a capstone project on traffic prediction.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: To contribute to AI solutions that improve daily life for citizens and, in the long run, shape national AI policy as a technologist.

initial_posts.narrative: As a research fellow collaborating with government agencies, I see NAIS 2.0 as a substantial commitment. The policy context states over S$500 million committed through AI Singapore for AI R&D, so I take that as the total funding. The co-funding cap is explicitly S$330,000 per 100E project. For compute, given the emphasis on industry use and the need for localised high-performance compute, I estimate around 5 million GPU hours allocated annually for AI experimentation, based on typical national AI infrastructure investments. For trainers, the AI Apprenticeship Programme and other upskilling efforts likely require a dedicated team of about 50 AI experts and trainers to guide enterprises and practitioners, considering the scale of the 100E programme and the goal to boost the AI practitioner pool to 15,000.

initial_posts.narrative: As a data scientist in logistics who has participated in the 100 Experiments programme, I see the impact phase as the culmination of the policy's inputs, activities, outputs, and outcomes. The substantial funding, compute, and training have led to 100 approved projects, 50 deployed, 12,000 practitioners, 100 generative AI use cases, and 25% enterprise adoption. These outcomes should improve Singapore's global AI readiness rank from around 6th to 5th, as the country demonstrates concrete AI deployment and talent development. The AI sector's GDP share should rise to about 3.5%, driven by increased enterprise adoption and new AI solutions. Public trust in AI systems is likely around 60%, as successful deployments and transparent governance build confidence, but concerns about job displacement and ethics remain. AI talent retention should be about 70%, as the programme's hands-on training and career opportunities encourage practitioners to stay, though some may be lured by global tech firms.
